notes: Fund data is state-level scheme/allocation data unless constituency-level work records are available from a public source.
Do not infer utilisation where the official source does not publish constituency-wise spend/work status.
Photos are loaded at runtime from Wikimedia Commons geocoded media using the constituency map centroid.

TN.scheme: Member of Legislative Assembly Constituency Development Scheme (MLACDS)
statewide: 2024-25: administrative sanction for Rs 702 Cr for 234 constituencies.
2023-24: Rs 702 Cr sanctioned; 9,012 works taken up at estimated cost of Rs 604.86 Cr; 1,384 completed and 7,628 in progress at the time of source publication.
2022-23: Rs 702 Cr released; 9,536 works taken up at estimated cost of Rs 683.55 Cr; 6,703 completed and 2,833 in progress.

KL.scheme: Legislative Assembly Constituency Asset Development Fund (LAC-ADF)
statewide: The scheme allocates Rs 5 Cr during a financial year for capital works in each Assembly constituency.
The source defines eligible durable assets such as buildings, roads, bridges, water supply, sanitation, irrigation, infrastructure, machinery, and equipment.

KA.scheme: Chief Minister's Infrastructure Development Programme / KLLAD context
KA.allocationLabel: 50 Cr announced per Assembly segment for 2025-26 infrastructure programme
statewide: Public reports say Rs 50 Cr was announced for each Assembly constituency under the Chief Minister's Infrastructure Development Programme for 2025-26.
Reported split: Rs 37.5 Cr for road, bridge, rural road/bridge, and urban-area works; Rs 12.5 Cr for other MLA-selected departmental works.
State ministers later clarified allocations may be based on developmental need rather than a mechanically uniform release.
workCategories: Public Works Department roads and bridges

TG.allocationLabel: Current per-constituency allocation not published in open source imported here
TG.financialYear: current open portal requires further source access
statewide: Telangana Planning Department lists Constituency Development Programme (MLAs/MLCs) among the programmes it implements.
The CLDMS portal exists for constituency local development monitoring, but the useful dashboard is login/captcha protected.
A published RTI-based report for 2014-15 to 2018-19 said Rs 1,400 Cr was released for MLAs and Rs 974.85 Cr spent in that period; this is historical, not a current constituency-level figure.
workCategories: District Collector / Planning Department monitored works

AP.scheme: State finance / constituency development budget tracking
AP.allocationLabel: Current per-constituency MLA fund source not publicly imported
statewide: The Andhra Pradesh Finance Department publishes budget releases, CBRO guidance, and district/constituency-level budget outreach notices.
A constituency-wise MLALAD/CDF work-status table was not found in the open sources imported for this dashboard.
Use this section as source-backed status rather than a fabricated utilisation value.
workCategories: Budget release and district-level allocation tracking
Constituency development works where district records publish them
Public infrastructure works from department or district notices
